---
title: Previewing
---

A preview renders the material locally and watches source documents for
changes. Use it while authoring so problems are found before content is
committed or published.

## Start with the documented task

Many repositories expose a Pixi task such as:

```bash
pixi run preview
```

Run it from the repository root unless the documentation says otherwise. The
task may set its own working directory and may install dependencies the first
time it runs.

## Common variants

The task signature depends on the project:

| Project shape | Example command | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| One website | `pixi run preview` | Preview the site's HTML output |
| Learner-facing and answer-bearing website | `pixi run preview exercise` or `pixi run preview solution` | Select a profile before previewing |
| Site with student/master profiles | `pixi run preview student` or `pixi run preview master` | Select the repository's terminology |
| Report or notebook set | `pixi run preview 1 student` | Select a document and profile |

These examples reflect existing course repositories; they are not interchangeable
commands. Check `README.md`, `pixi.toml`, or `pyproject.toml` for the accepted
arguments in the repository being edited.

## While previewing

Edit the `.qmd` source document, save it, and reload or wait for the preview to
update. Check:

- Headings, equations, figures, links, callouts, and downloadable resources.
- Executable cells or interactive components when the project uses them.
- Both learner-facing and answer-bearing variants when content visibility may
  change between them.

Do not edit the HTML emitted in `_site/` or another generated output directory;
the next preview or render will replace it.
